upcoming.label: Next Meeting
upcoming.title: Ethical Dilemmas: Decisions That Will Define the Future
upcoming.description: From self-driving cars to AI in medicine and warfare — we'll tackle the dilemmas that don't have easy answers. A structured discussion on the choices humanity will be forced to make.
keyQuestions: Who is morally responsible when an autonomous system causes harm?
Should AI ever make life-or-death decisions without human override?
How do we program ethics into machines when humans disagree on ethics?
Is there a meaningful difference between AI causing harm and a human using AI to cause harm?
upcoming.suggestedReading: Nathan Dozois's article on the Anthropic-Pentagon partnership is directly relevant to this meeting.

past.title: Advanced Seminar: AI Dilemmas
past.description: A deep dive into the real ethical and societal tensions in AI — from autonomous decision-making to alignment challenges.
